yt_dlp_core.title: Video by hiddenjemmmig
yt_dlp_core.description:
One of the best SUPPER and dinner spots in Singapore! 🤌🏼 I had my first ever frog 🐸 leg porridge here and never looked back! Close to 10x here and the food is always good!! What you should order: 
🥣 Claypot Porridge 
🐸 Claypot Frogleg 
🥚 Cai Po Omelette 
🥗 Kailan / Kangkong 
🤤 Sambal Stingray (its not very spicy!) 
📌 Hong Chang Frog Porridge and BBQ Fish (2 Braddell Rd, Singapore 359895) #sghiddengems #sghiddenfood #sgfoodie #sgsupper #sgfood #sgwheretoeat #sgwhattoeat #sgeats #sgmakandiary

sample.author: sufyb.realtor
sample.text: This place looks like a hidden gem for supper! 🍲 The Claypot Porridge and Frog Leg are definitely intriguing, I can see why you never looked back!

address_like_snippets: 📌 Hong Chang Frog Porridge and BBQ Fish (2 Braddell Rd, Singapore 359895)

yt_dlp_core.title: Video by eshton
yt_dlp_core.description:
I absolutely loved the flavours here! Combining Spanish food with his own unique experience, Chef @gonzaloland serves dishes that are both homely and luxurious! 

My personal favourites were of course the Beef Short Rib, which was extremely tender, and the Hokkaido Scallops with Foie Gras for those delicious umami flavours! 

But one of the most interesting dishes was their own variation of paella! The Foie Gras & Fig Rice combines those rich buttery notes with perfectly al dente golden rice to create a dish that was delicious!

📍 NÓMADA, 1 Keong Saik Rd., 01-05, Singapore 089109

#sgfoodie #wheretoeat #sgrestaurant #datenight #paella #foiegras #shortribs #scallops #beef #eshton

sample.author: sufyb.realtor
sample.text: Those short ribs though!!❤️ The ambience of this place and the dishes the serve - awesome🙌❤️

sample.author: jsnjnr
sample.text: The short ribs spoke to me 🤤

address_like_snippets: 📍 NÓMADA, 1 Keong Saik Rd., 01-05, Singapore 089109

transcription.text: If you love Fwagra scallops and beef, you have to come to no matter. They're located in Kyeongsai Road with chef Gonzalo putting his own spin on traditional Spanish food. Serving up things like grilled oysters with spicy soba sada, and a homemade flatbread with cheese and a whole bunch of herbaceous flavors. For one of the starters, you have to try as their ox dill croquets. For vegetables, their asparagus and brussels sprouts comes with poached egg and miso butter for the extra bit of creamy texture. We also tried their squids with lemon and parsley sauce, and it was an easy-resistible combination of refreshing and smoky. And their Hokkaido scallops come with a generous amount of Fwagra, and it's interestingly combined with vanilla and parsnip puree. But I'm always a sucker for melt in your mouth, braised short ribs, and hand-de-deliver on this. The meat was so tender, it literally fell right apart. And for one of the most overt-at-top versions of Paya that I've had, the Fwagra and Figs Rice combines their rich buttery umami with a perfectly marinated golden rice. So share this with someone you have to come here with.

yt_dlp_core.title: Video by selfcarelivingdays
yt_dlp_core.description:
I used to think paying for Japanese food is super not worth it until @ganko.sushi_sg 

It is one of the BEST Japanese restaurants in Singapore I’ve been to 🍣❤️✨

They apparently have their ingredients airflown from Japan 3x a week, so it’s super fresh and of high quality 🤤 

It’s not cheap, but you get what you pay for and I think it’s a great place to splurge on for special occasions ❤️

My favourites were:
🐟Truffle Sashimi Don 8.8/10 - super fresh fish and filled with premium ingredients like freshly shaved truffle, uni and caviar 🤩 incredibly luxurious 

🍣 Premium Nigiri Platter 8.6/10: super fresh, sweet & clean tasting sashimi slices!!! It’s the first time I feel knife skills matter 😜 really very smooth, buttery tuna slices 👍🏻

🥪 Wagyu Sando 8.2/10: Wagyu leaner cut but still has that rich, buttery fragrance - good if you’re not into that creamy fatty taste that typical Wagyu has ✨

Overall, a great place for dates and special occasions ❤️

📍Ganko Sushi
9 Penang Rd, 01-01, Singapore 238459

#sg #sgfoodies #japanesefood #sashimi #sushi

address_like_snippets: 9 Penang Rd, 01-01, Singapore 238459

transcription.text: I think Japanese food is a surprise. I think I tried this restaurant in Dobi-Guard that says premium, fresh and quality ingredients. The wakizando was tender and fatty and this luxurious bowl of Trafordon hit super fresh seafood. The ingredients are flown in from Japan three times a week so your bet is gotta be super fresh. I love how Japanese cuisine is able to highlight the freshness and quality of the ingredients and ganko sushi does it really well here. Make sure you see if you can share for your perfect eat.

transcription.text: I finally tried out this famous Thai restaurant in Pijay and I must say it didn't disappoint. It's called Yen Wautai and it serve authentic northern Thai food in a cozy ambience with an open kitchen. I tried the Tom Yum going with huge prawns and it was probably the first time at such thick Tom Yum soup. There was so much flavour. Then I had the soft shell crab with curry egg which was my favourite of the night. I tried fish with lots of lemongrass which was nice but was a bit too sour for me. A braised pork leg which had a nice mix of lean and melt in your mouth meat. And if you like pork skin, the braised pig kale was really nice too. I also like that the service was good and food came fast. Follow me from all good food in KL. Thank you.

It's called Yen Wautai and it serve authentic northern Thai food in a cozy ambience with an open kitchen. I tried the Tom Yum going with huge prawns and it was probably the first time at such thick Tom Yum soup. There was so much flavour. Then I had the soft shell crab with curry egg which was my favourite of the night. I tried fish with lots of lemongrass which was nice but was a bit too sour for me. A braised pork leg which had a nice mix of lean and melt in your mouth meat. And if you like pork skin, the braised pig kale was really nice too. I also like that the service was good and food came fast. Follow me from all good food in KL. Thank you.

yt_dlp_core.title: Post by districtsixtyfive
yt_dlp_core.description:
Our favourite dining spots in 2025 that deserves a chance on your 2026 food list 😋

🍝 Osteria Mozza

5-course pasta meal and every dish slapped

🐄 GU:UM

Modern Korean steakhouse

🍲 Uncle Fong Hotpot

One of the freshest ingredients we’ve had, especially their kampong chicken

🍣 Sushi Zushi

“Omakase-style” sushi bar (bro the queue is v long)

🐥 The Plump Frenchman

Came for their roast chicken, but man, was their Lemon Tiramisu and Chocolate Mousse good

🍚 Vios by Blu Kouzina

Mediterranean grain bowl at Raffles City 

🍔 Bochinche

Argentinian steakhouse with an underrated burger

👀 Follow @districtsixtyfive for the best things to eat in Singapore

#sgfoodies #exploresg

yt_dlp_core.title: Video by caitofalltraits
yt_dlp_core.description:
Would you go to Damansara, KL just for this?

People often think local food is limited to whatever originated from a country, but as a Singaporean, I know that’s not true. There are local takes on so many different cuisines from around the world - and thank goodness for that!

Which is why I hate the use of the word ‘authentic’ to describe food (I have a story about this that I will share when I’m in NYC this March), but other than that, I love being able to enjoy all sorts of cuisines done well, like this one particular dish from @uokatsu.malaysia 

📍 Malaysia, Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur, Kuala Lumpur, Sri Hartamas, Jalan Sri Hartamas 1, B-0-7, Ground Floor Plaza Damas 3

Enjoy!
yt_dlp_core.uploader: Caitanya Tan | Actor • Presenter • Voiceover Artist • Creator

address_like_snippets: 📍 Malaysia, Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur, Kuala Lumpur, Sri Hartamas, Jalan Sri Hartamas 1, B-0-7, Ground Floor Plaza Damas 3

transcription.text: One of the perks of being Singaporean is definitely our proximity to Malaysia. It's freaking crazy, bud. Because after we're done exploring the touristy foods, we can anytime come back for more local recommendations. Like this place, twice I mentioned where I was staying to my local friends. Twice I was told to go to the same restaurant and both mentioned but one dish. I ordered a cabbage salad, teriyaki card and some sashimi and they were all yummy. But the one dish, the one that's in nearly every photo on their Google reviews, perfectly listening Japanese rice topped with tamago yaki slices that add sweetness to the umami rich creamy-touched men-tikomeo for friends, Malaysians, countrymen. lend me your ears because this is the dish that made me buy an aburi torch off of Amazon. Save one of your meals for this. I'd never lie about these things and you can thank me later.

yt_dlp_core.title: Video by ericnyker
yt_dlp_core.description:
📍Pazonia Italian Street Food @ Amoy
49 Amoy St, Singapore 069875

Guys @pazonia.sg made me feel that I’ve overpaid for western sandwiches the entire time , not to mention subway…

I could literally eat the Panuozzo Carne everyday lol. Get the burrata to indulge a bit it’s seriously so perfect with the Parma ham. 

@pazonia.sg also has a promo going on - for week night dinners- you get the 2nd pizza for 50% off ! It’s from March 3 - 14 :)

sample.text: Oh man I missed the deal

address_like_snippets: 49 Amoy St, Singapore 069875
📍Pazonia Italian Street Food @ Amoy

transcription.text: for under $10. Literally you can swap your type and come here and eat this. Wow. Guys, after eating at this place, Panzonia. I feel that I have overpaid for Western sandwiches in Singapore the entire time. Oh, really? For 12? For 12 90? Would you go to South-Bab? I wouldn't. You know why? Everything in there imported by Italy. Oh, yeah. I don't know. I don't know how many. South-Bab is the only one who can eat. So yeah. Here at Panzonia, they're focused on the sea Talent Street food for Panozzo. They are pretty much Italian sandwiches using fake-to-order South-Dope Pizza Pie. If you like Italian meat cuts, the panozzo carne with the meat torch and charred dough flavors. Just taste the Italian BMT to another level. To indulge a bit more, get the brata and parmahem one. And yes, you get the whole bowl of imported brata all to yourself. Another item we absolutely loved was the signature Panzonia pizza. With a spicy Arabada sauce made in-house and fanaties. Oh, by the way, the pizzas are 50% off the second one. So check my caption for details.

SOLID SANDWICH & BAKES SPOT

Will never gatekeep places so here’s another sandwich find which comes close to the one i had at beth’s 

📍Baker’s Bench Bakery, Bukit Pasoh Road 

Affordable pricing and a wide variety of bakes!!

The sandwich here uses ciabatta and crushed pistachio, which differs from Beth that uses pistachio butter and focaccia. 

#cafe #food #restaurant #eat

yt_dlp_core.title: Video by piggweee
yt_dlp_core.description:
SG's BEST sundubu⁉️ handmade tofu with rich, nutty flavour 😍 SAVE & SHARE this for your next Korean food cravings! 😱

NEW Korean restaurant by the popular Um Yong Baek serves freshly-made tofu with refillable barley rice, sesame oil, seaweed & side dishes! 🤩 

definitely add this to your must-try list if you love Korean food! 📌 

✨️ what we've tried:
Gosari Sundubu Jjigae 고사리 순두부찌개 - Soft Tofu Stew with Bracken ($24)
Saewoo Sundubu Jjigae 새우 순두부찌개 - Soft Tofu Stew with Shrimp ($24)
Nokdu Bindae-tteok 녹두 빈대떡 - Mung Bean Pancake ($13)

yt_dlp_core.title: Post by ivyluvv.travel
yt_dlp_core.description:
What to eat in Kuala Lumpur? EVERYTHING! 🤭💛
I spent 5 days eating non-stop and still craving more!

When in Malaysia, don’t miss out on Nasi Lemak or Nasi Kandar, true local legends!🔥 Bold flavours, spicy, and absolutely delicious 😍

Here are all the must-try food spots in KL! save this post so you don’t miss any when you visit! 🇲🇾🔥

what to eat in kuala lumpur | KL Food | #klfood #malaysianfood #klfoodies

yt_dlp_core.title: Video by thecassiefeed
yt_dlp_core.description:
‼️SAVE THIS‼️ for your next trip to Kuala Lumpur/Selangor! 

I’ve been to KL countless times. Each time, I discover new spots to try, here are the ones that made the list this trip. Malaysia is definitely a foodies paradise. 

📍 Huen Kee Claypot Chicken Rice

59, Jln Yew, Pudu, 55100 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia
(Closed on Thursdays)

📍 Restoran Kin Kin - famous KL Chilli Pan Mee 🌶️

No. 6, Jalan Perubatan 4, Taman, Pandan Indah, 55100 Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan, Malaysia

📍 Lam Kee Wantan Noodles - Char Siu & Char Siu Pork Rib Wantan Noodles 🍜 

Jalan Bunga Tanjung 10A, Taman Muda, 56100 Kuala Lumpur, Selangor, Malaysia
(Closed on Tuesdays)

📍 大树下炸年糕 Under The Big Tree Fried Nian Gao (not on Google maps)

Opposite Restoran Hong Sang: D22, Gerai Staik, Jalan Bunga Tanjung 10, Taman Muda, 68000 Ampang, Selangor, Malaysia

📍 Nasi Lemak Shop

G10, Oasis Business Center BU4, Changkat Bandar Utama, Bandar Utama, 47800 Petaling Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

📍 汤圆坊 YUAN (巴生港口汤圆 • 麻薯 @ Taman Paramount)

40, Jalan 20/16a, Taman Paramount, 46300 Petaling Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia
(Closed on Sundays)

If you like what you see, follow me for more!

#klfood #kualalumpurfood #localfood #wheretoeatkl #whattoeatkl #klfoodie #malaysia #malaysiafood

transcription.text: Six local spots to try when you're in Kuala Lumpur. Starting with a Cantonese specialty, Huyung Kui, Claypot, Chicken Rice, and Pudu. It was flavourful, fragrant, and full of walk-through. Do also try the Haka Yellow Wine Chicken. It was boozy and warming, not for the faint hearted. If you like your chili bun meat, you definitely have to get your fix at restaurant Kin Kin. It literally appears on every guidebook and I can see why. It's the perfect formula of outstanding noodles, means pork and a lot of crunchy chili flakes. Just be aware that the chili can be really spicy even for us. It's a spicy and you like me. You probably had a lot of good tassue, but have you had tassue pot ribs? Kate over to Lunky Wonton Noodles to try their delicious taste of tassue and tassue pot ribs, one can noodles. We actually preferred the tassue to the tassue pot ribs, but we saw extra portions of the pot ribs on every other table. So you decide. Right across the road of Lunky Wonton Noodles, we stumbled upon a stall selling fried yanko. Nyanca is a sticky chewy brown sugar mochi, and despite our heavy breakfast, we couldn't resist the tree in one which comes with sweet potato, yank and the miencao, battered and deep fried. It was so fresh and so good. We wish we had room for more. When in KL, you can't live without the Malaysian national dish, Nassilamak. I had my fill at the Nassilamak shop, and they beat Village Park any time of the day, really controversial, but that's my take. The true winner was the fried chicken. The batter was incredible. That being said, I wish to use better rice. And lastly for dessert, be sure to try the fresh tea hand-made tangyuan and mochi. Only open from 6pm to 10pm at Tamil Paramau. Save this post and follow me for more.

It was so fresh and so good. We wish we had room for more. When in KL, you can't live without the Malaysian national dish, Nassilamak. I had my fill at the Nassilamak shop, and they beat Village Park any time of the day, really controversial, but that's my take. The true winner was the fried chicken. The batter was incredible. That being said, I wish to use better rice. And lastly for dessert, be sure to try the fresh tea hand-made tangyuan and mochi. Only open from 6pm to 10pm at Tamil Paramau. Save this post and follow me for more.
